Video.summary: TFH celebrates Alfred Hitchcock and the masterful storytelling that still keeps audiences on the edge of their seats.
Video.title: Episode 1

Video.summary: From monster mashups and horror hijinks, Bela Lugosi was much more than cinema’s greatest vampire.
Video.title: Episode 1

Video.summary: Looking back at the iconic films of horror director George A. Romero.
Video.title: Episode 1

Video.summary: From 'Haunted Hill' to Corman classics, Vincent Price sent shivers down the spines of horror fans for decades.
Video.grandparentTitle: Vincent Price: Master of the Silver Scream
Video.title: Episode 1

Video.summary: From gunslingers to rebel heroes, Clint Eastwood brings grit, charm, and intensity in a way that no other movie star can.
Video.title: Episode 1

Video.summary: John Phillip Law and Lee Van Cleef aren’t friends, but they’ll find they have something in common when they both set out to hunt down a gang of vicious outlaws in the Wild West. Directed by Giulio Petroni, this 1967 Spaghetti Western features a musical score by the legendary Ennio Morricone.
Video.title: Death Rides a Horse

Video.summary: From Red River to Rio Bravo, outlaws and legends collide in some of the greatest Westerns ever made.
Video.title: Episode 1

Video.summary: These Western classics capture a fading frontier, where aging outlaws and a changing world bring the Wild West to its final reckoning.
Video.title: Episode 1

Video.summary: From Dollars to Django and beyond, Italian westerns captured the frontier with style, scale, and unforgettable antiheroes.
Video.title: Episode 1

Video.summary: These Hollywood Westerns looked beyond legend, showing audiences the harsh reality of frontier conflict.
Video.title: Episode 1

Video.summary: From sharp satire to offbeat stories, these Westerns put a fresh spin on Hollywood's wildest frontier.
Video.title: Episode 1

Video.summary: A legend in the exploitation genre, these are the films that define director Jack Hill's career.
Video.title: Episode 1

Video.summary: Bikers, Babes, and Badass ACTION: Revisiting the best of 60s and 70s cult cinema.
Video.title: Episode 1

Video.summary: Stay off the streets when these Hollywood stars get behind the wheel.
Video.title: Episode 1

Video.summary: Bold and seductive. Provocative and iconic. These boundary-pushing movies live on the edge.
Video.title: Episode 1

Video.summary: From Hammer hits to Corman classics and more, these movies made Christopher Lee a horror icon.
Video.grandparentTitle: Christopher Lee: From Fangs to Frights
Video.title: Episode 1

Video.summary: Your favorite filmmakers look back at the movies influenced by famous literary works.
Video.grandparentTitle: From the Page to the Screen Vol. 1
Video.title: Episode 1

Video.summary: Dive deeper into the classic books that became Hollywood blockbusters!
Video.grandparentTitle: From the Page to the Screen Vol. 2
Video.title: Episode 1

Video.summary: From Stephen King to Kurt Vonnegut, Hollywood brings your favorite books to the big screen!
Video.grandparentTitle: From the Page to the Screen Vol. 3
Video.title: Episode 1

Video.summary: Football legend Joe Namath steps off the field and in front of the camera for this 1970 action picture directed by television veteran Seymour Robbie. After crossing paths with a dangerous gang in the desert, biker C.C. Ryder (Namath) must rescue the lovely fashion journalist Ann (Ann-Margaret) from their clutches.
Video.title: CC & Company

Video.summary: The infamous outlaw Jesse James crosses paths with Dr. Frankenstein's murderous granddaughter, Maria.
Video.title: Jesse James Meets Frankensteins Daughter

Video.summary: While “The Raven” may fly high, others less fortunate are subject to a “Premature Burial” inside “The Tomb of Ligeia”
Video.title: Corman, Price and Poe

Video.summary: A down-on-his-luck Humphrey Bogart teams with a group of crooks chasing their fortune in British East Africa.
Video.title: Beat the Devil

Video.summary: Frank Sinatra was nominated for an Academy Award for Best Actor for his portrayal of Frankie Machine, a former drug addict struggling with sobriety after he’s released from prison. Though Frankie dreams of a music career, he finds himself caught up in a web of lies, betrayal, and murder that could eventually lead to his downfall. Based on the novel by Nelson Algren, The Man With the Golden Arm was added to the National Film Registry by the Library of Congress in 2020.
Video.grandparentTitle: The Man with the Golden Arm
Video.title: Episode 1

Video.summary: From "The Big Sleep" to "Casablanca" and beyond, this is the best of Humphrey Bogart.
Video.title: Episode 1

Video.summary: Loyalty, vengeance, and justice collide in these Western hits starring Glenn Ford, Steve McQueen, and more.
Video.title: Episode 1

Video.summary: Blending classic Western roots with modern storytelling, these films reveal a frontier that never ends.
Video.grandparentTitle: Modern Outlaws and Ghosts of the West
Video.title: Episode 1

Video.summary: Take a high-octane ride through director Jonathan Kaplan's world of renegades, road warriors, and the rough edges of the 1970s.
Video.grandparentTitle: Hard Turns and Hard Times: The Films of Jonathan Kaplan
Video.title: Episode 1

Video.summary: Step into the world of horror and sci-fi master John Carpenter with this spine-tingling look at some of his most celebrated films.
Video.title: Episode 1
